spec_match.rationale: Uses the WICG-shaped readonly numeric navigator.cpuPerformance attribute with a tier fallback when the origin trial is unavailable.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP clicked tier 0 through 4 and observed static, fade, slide, orbit, and 20-particle outputs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index and concept page both describe tier-selected animation complexity.

edge_cases.rationale: prefers-reduced-motion is layered on top of the selected tier, forcing the effective output to tier 0 instead of allowing high-motion overrides.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables for colors and keeps button background/color pairs explicit.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh Chrome DevTools MCP v152 sweep loaded the copied concept, exercised every detected control, found named controls, and reported no post-triage runtime/action failure.

conformance.rationale: A v152 suite now carries the same feature-ID/spec contract without modifying the immutable source-milestone suite.

summary: Five tiered animation presets now read navigator.cpuPerformance as a numeric attribute and keep prefers-reduced-motion as a hard override. Chrome DevTools MCP found no console errors.
